name: Jin Xuan Oolong
region: Grown across Taiwan — Ali Shan, Nantou, Mingjian
terroir: Adaptable cultivar; best examples from moderate-to-high elevation (600–1200m)
flavor_profile: Naturally creamy and smooth with a distinctive milky sweetness that comes from the cultivar itself, not added flavoring. Higher levels of natural lactones produce a buttery, custard-like character with gentle floral undertones. Minimal astringency makes it exceptionally approachable. Beware cheap artificially flavored versions — genuine Jin Xuan is subtler.

brewing.tips: Standard ball-rolled oolong parameters. The creamy character fades if over-steeped — keep it tight and enjoy the milk notes across many rounds.
